methodology: Cross-reference network (Isaiah 64:8 hidden hub, 55 crossrefs) + patristic reading (Chrysostom bridges 2Co4:7→Jdg7→1Sam17; Augustine massa damnata 5 entries; Jerome dual treasure) + JFB cross-refs + 4-context vocabulary mapping

evidence: commentaries (Chrysostom cites Gideon+David, Lapide, Jerome, Clement, Tertullian — 8 entries)
commentaries (Augustine massa damnata 5 entries, Chrysostom, Tertullian — 10 entries)

new_refs_added.justification: Hidden hub: 9v de JER.18.6, 7v de ROM.9.21, 8v→GEN.2.7, 55 total crossrefs. 'We are the clay, you are our potter.' Connects ALL 4 contexts.
Vessel RESHAPED: 'marred in his hand, he reworked it.' 6v→ISA.64.8, 5v→ROM.9.20. Dynamic element: clay re-formed, not discarded.

candidates_noted.justification: 6v de ROM.9.20. 'Woe to the one who contends with the Creator!'
5v de ROM.9.21. Vessels of honor/dishonor in a great house.
6v de 2CO.4.7. 'Precious sons of Zion regarded as clay pots.'

scholarly_notes.chrysostom_bridge: On 2Co 4:7 explicitly connects: apostles (earthen vessels) = Gideon's 300 (pitchers) = David vs Goliath. Same principle: divine power through human fragility.
scholarly_notes.augustine_massa_damnata: All humanity as condemned clay in Adam. Vessels of wrath = deserved penalty; vessels of mercy = undeserved grace.
scholarly_notes.isaiah_64_8_hub: Hidden hub with 55 crossrefs connecting ALL contexts. Not in original gold set but essential.
